title: carphephorus odoratissimus var. subtropicanus
intro: Carphephorus odoratissimus var. subtropicanus, also known as the subtropical carphephorus, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, characterized by its fragrant leaves and purple flowers. The subtropical carphephorus has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of the subtropical carphephorus often involve decoctions or infusions of its leaves, roots, or flowers, which can be used to create teas, salves, or other remedies.
